title: Newton's Three Laws of Motion
subject: physics
summary: Three short physical demos, one per law, laid out left-to-right on the studio stage so a learner can compare them at a glance.

beats.subtitle: Inertia
beats.title: An object in motion stays in motion
beats.narration: With nothing to slow it down, the puck keeps gliding at the same speed forever. That tendency to keep doing what it's already doing is inertia.

beats.subtitle: F = ma
beats.title: Force equals mass times acceleration
beats.narration: Gravity pulls the ball down the ramp. The steeper the slope, the larger the force, and the faster the ball speeds up. Watch the gaps grow as it accelerates.

beats.subtitle: Action & Reaction
beats.title: Every action has an equal and opposite reaction
beats.narration: Release the spring and both carts shove off each other. The push is equal both ways, so the lighter cart leaves faster than the heavy one.

_note: Declarative storyboard. Director owns narration/order; Artist owns `demo` + `params` (the physics it builds in Blender); Developer owns nothing here — it stages by rule via xr_design.
